W00001.notes: Compound noun (this morning). Special reading けさ (NOT いまあさ). Member of a small set of irregular-reading time words: 今日 (きょう), 今夜 (こんや), 今晩 (こんばん), 昨日 (きのう), 明日 (あした).

W00006.notes: Ichidan verb. Dictionary form 見る. Te-form 見て. Past 見た / 見ました. Used 11+ times — heaviest verb in library.

W00007.notes: Noun (tree). Reading き. Same kanji 木 also reads もく in some compounds (木曜日 = Thursday).

W00008.notes: Stored in dictionary form (濡れる) rather than polite -ます form because this verb is only ever used in te-form across stories so far (濡れて). Most other verbs are stored in -ます form for consistency.

W00010.notes: Godan verb (drink). Dict form 飲む. Polite past 飲みました. Honorific お飲みになる (out of scope).

W00011.notes: na-adjective (quiet). Pairs with です directly (静かです), but requires な before a noun (静かな猫). Most-used adjective in library.

W00012.notes: i-adjective (warm). Ends in い, conjugates: 温かい/温かくない/温かかった. Distinct from 暖かい (also "warm", but for weather rather than objects/feelings).

W00013.notes: i-adjective (good). Irregular: 良い/良くない/良かった (the "i" reverts to "yo" in non-present forms). Use いい (informal) in predicate position; 良い (formal/written) attributively.

W00017.notes: Godan verb (walk). Dict form 歩く. Te-form 歩いて. Compound 歩き出す (start walking) is N3.

W00020.notes: Ichidan verb (eat). Dict form 食べる. Te-form 食べて. Past 食べた / 食べました. Polite negative 食べません.

W00023.notes: Noun (a walk). Forms a verb with します: 散歩します (to take a walk). In our stories so far it appears as a noun. The verb form would need a separate W-id and verb_class=irregular_suru.

W00027.notes: Noun (wind). Reading かぜ. Same kanji 風 also reads ふう meaning "style/manner" (洋風 = Western style).

W00026.notes: Godan verb (return home/go back). Dict form 帰る. Te-form 帰って. Distinct from 返る (to be returned, intransitive).

W00025.notes: Loanword (door, from English). Always written in katakana. Refers to Western-style doors (hinged); a Japanese sliding door is 戸 (と) or 障子 (しょうじ).

W00029.notes: Ichidan verb of EXISTENCE for ANIMATE things (people, animals). Inanimate counterpart is あります W00044. Use いる/います for what can move; ある/あります for what cannot. Common pairing: subject + が + います.

W00031.notes: Noun (moon). Reading つき. Same kanji 月 also reads がつ (counter for months: 一月 ichigatsu = January) and げつ (Monday: 月曜日 getsuyoubi). Context disambiguates.

W00035.notes: Counter for people (two people). Special reading ふたり (NOT にひと). Same irregular pattern as 一人 (ひとり). For 3+ people, use the regular counter -人 with にん reading: 三人 (さんにん).

W00033.notes: Noun (book). Reading ほん. Distinct from 本 (もと) meaning "origin" — same kanji, totally different word.

W00034.notes: Godan verb (read). Dict form 読む. Stored in polite -ます form (読みます) matching all other -ます-headword verbs. Te-form 読んで.

W00038.notes: Ichidan verb (sleep). Dict form 寝る. Te-form 寝て. Distinct from 眠る (to fall asleep, more poetic).

W00036.notes: Noun (chair). Reading いす. Often written in hiragana too: いす. Both are correct.

W00037.notes: Noun (desk). Reading つくえ. Compare 卓 (たく, table) — same general category, different word.

W00040.notes: Irregular verb (来る kuru = "to come"). Conjugates as: 来ます (come), 来ました (came), 来て (te-form), 来ない (negative). The kanji reading changes by form: 来る (くる), 来ます (きます), 来ない (こない). Test cases for the verb engine.

W00041.notes: Godan verb (wait). Dict form 待つ. Te-form 待って. Past 待ちました.

W00042.notes: Time noun (yesterday). Special reading きのう (NOT さくじつ in conversation, though 昨日 (さくじつ) exists in formal/written contexts). Member of the same irregular-reading time-words set as W00001.

W00043.notes: Godan verb (think). Dict form 思う. Used almost exclusively in と思います construction (G014) — quoted-thought reporting. Plain と思う (less polite) is identical in meaning.

W00044.notes: Irregular verb of EXISTENCE for INANIMATE things (objects, plants, abstract). Animate counterpart is います W00029. The "irregularity" is in the negative (ありません, NOT あらない) and plain past (あった, NOT あらった). Stored as verb_class=irregular_aru.
